title: Ranunculus lyngei
taxonomy: Ranunculus lyngei is a species of flowering plant in the family Ranunculaceae. It belongs to the genus Ranunculus, which includes approximately 600 species of buttercups and their relatives. Ranunculus lyngei is a member of the Ranunculales order and the Ranunculaceae family. This classification places Ranunculus lyngei within the larger group of flowering plants known as angiosperms. The plant's specific classification is further defined by its unique characteristics and genetic makeup.

intro: Ranunculus lyngei, also known as the Arctic buttercup, is a perennial plant species native to the Arctic tundra. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may aid in reducing pain and improving overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Ranunculus lyngei include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its potential therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Ranunculus lyngei is commonly prepared as a tincture or tea, and may also be used in topical applications to take advantage of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
